steps.title: Convert fractional exponents to radical notation
Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: $\dfrac{\sqrt{y}}{\sqrt[3]{x^2}}$. This gives $y^{1/2} / x^{2/3}$, but we need $y^{3/2} / x^{7/3}$. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: $\dfrac{y\sqrt{y}}{\sqrt[3]{x^2}}$. The numerator is correct, but the denominator is $x^{2/3}$, not $x^{7/3}$. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: $\dfrac{y\sqrt{y}}{x\sqrt{x}}$. The denominator is $x^{3/2}$, but we need $x^{7/3}$. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: $\dfrac{y\sqrt{y}}{x^2 \sqrt[3]{x}}$. The denominator is $x^2 \cdot x^{1/3} = x^{7/3}$, which matches exactly. That matches choice D — D is correct.
